criterion: Contains no debug print statements or dead code in newly added scripts checkpoint.gd, level_exit.gd, and game_manager.gd.

forms.assessment_process: Search those scripts for print calls and commented out or unreachable blocks. Confirm only intentional logging remains.
forms.explanation: Leftover debug code and dead code reduce maintainability and can cause noisy output or hidden behavior in production.

criterion: Demonstrates a reproducible run that reaches level_2.tscn and shows score persistence and checkpoint respawn.

forms.assessment_process: Run from the menu into level 1, activate a checkpoint, change score, touch the exit, then die in level 2 and confirm respawn and score.
forms.explanation: End to end validation catches integration bugs across scenes, UI, and autoload state that unit checks can miss.

criterion: Shows level_2.tscn with a distinct platform arrangement and at least one traversal segment not present in level_1.tscn.

forms.assessment_process: Compare level_1.tscn and level_2.tscn in the editor and confirm the main platform path and obstacle placement are not reused.
forms.explanation: A distinct layout ensures the second level provides new gameplay value rather than a duplicate scene with minor edits.

criterion: Includes in level_2.tscn a spawn point, 2+ checkpoints, a level exit, 1+ of each enemy type, collectibles, and a power up.

forms.assessment_process: Open level_2.tscn and verify required nodes or instanced scenes exist and are reachable in play. Confirm at least one of each enemy type is present.
forms.explanation: Minimum content requirements ensure level 2 is playable and exercises the reused gameplay systems.

criterion: Loads level_2.tscn when the player enters level_exit.tscn, and the previous level root is freed without errors.

forms.assessment_process: Enter the exit in level 1, confirm the active scene is level_2.tscn, and check the debugger for errors and duplicate level roots.
forms.explanation: Clean scene transitions prevent memory leaks, duplicated gameplay nodes, and state bugs during level changes.

criterion: Includes 2 to 3 checkpoint instances placed along the main traversal path in level_1.tscn.

forms.assessment_process: Open level_1.tscn and locate checkpoint instances. Confirm they are positioned on the intended route rather than stacked at spawn.
forms.explanation: Checkpoint placement affects usability and ensures the feature is exercised during normal play.

criterion: Respawns the player at the last activated checkpoint position when the player dies.

forms.assessment_process: Activate a checkpoint, trigger the existing death condition, and confirm the player spawns at the checkpoint location.
forms.explanation: Checkpoint respawn is the core gameplay requirement and must work reliably under the existing death flow.

criterion: Keeps only one checkpoint active, and reverts the previous checkpoint to its inactive color on activation.

forms.assessment_process: Activate checkpoint A then checkpoint B, confirm B shows active color and A returns to inactive color.
forms.explanation: Single active checkpoint prevents ambiguous respawn targets and provides consistent player feedback.

criterion: Activates a checkpoint on player contact, changes its color, and updates the respawn position to that checkpoint.

forms.assessment_process: Touch an inactive checkpoint, confirm its visual color changes, then die and confirm respawn occurs at that checkpoint.
forms.explanation: Immediate feedback and correct respawn targeting are essential for player understanding and functional checkpoints.

criterion: Includes checkpoint.tscn with an Area2D and CollisionShape2D used to detect the player.

forms.assessment_process: Open checkpoint.tscn and verify it contains an Area2D with a configured CollisionShape2D. Confirm the player can trigger it in play.
forms.explanation: A consistent detection setup prevents missed triggers and ensures checkpoints work reliably across levels.

criterion: Resets GameManager score to 0 when Restart is used, and the score label shows 0 after reload.

forms.assessment_process: Gain score, press Restart, then confirm GameManager.score is 0 and the UI score label displays 0.
forms.explanation: Restart should reliably clear persistent session state so players do not carry score into a new run.

criterion: Preserves GameManager.score across the level 1 to level 2 transition, and the score label matches after load.

forms.assessment_process: Set a nonzero score in level 1, transition to level 2, then confirm GameManager.score and the UI label show the same value.
forms.explanation: Persistent score is a core requirement of the transition and validates the autoload and UI binding are correct.

criterion: Uses GameManager as the only score store, and UI reads the displayed score directly from GameManager.score.

forms.assessment_process: Search scripts for a score variable outside game_manager.gd and for direct UI owned score state. Verify score label binds to GameManager value.
forms.explanation: Centralizing score prevents desync across scenes and simplifies persistence and reset behavior.

criterion: Registers game_manager.gd as an AutoLoad singleton, and scripts access it via the global GameManager name.

forms.assessment_process: Check Project Settings AutoLoad list for GameManager, then open a script that reads or writes GameManager.score without node paths.
forms.explanation: Autoload registration is required for score persistence and avoids fragile scene tree dependencies.

criterion: Opens and runs with level_1.tscn as main scene, and contains no remaining references to platformer.tscn.

forms.assessment_process: Run the project and confirm the main scene is level_1.tscn. Search the project for "platformer.tscn" and confirm no hits remain.
forms.explanation: Broken scene paths are a common regression after renames and can prevent the game from booting or loading levels.
